name: Pad Thai with Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the rice noodles in cold tap water about 20 minutes until they are "springy." Drain in a colander until needed.
2. If using dried tamarind, soak it in hot water for awhile, then mash with a fork to soften. Force as much of the mixture through a sieve to remove bits of bark, etc.
3. Heat oil in a wok and add the tamarind/tomato sauce, pickled radish, and sugar. Mix well until heated.
4. Add the noodles, small portions at a time, and water/stock. Mix well until all noodles are coated with the mixture. If needed, add more liquid to cook out; avoid being too gentle on the noodles. Toss them like a salad for better coating.
5. Beat the egg and mix with the noodles. Add fish sauce and shrimp. Mix everything thoroughly. The noodles may clump, so stir or toss like a salad to combine everything and ensure thorough cooking of the egg and shrimp. Cover with a lid briefly, then toss and cover again until the shrimp are completely pink.
6. Add bean sprouts, green onions, and chopped peanuts. Mix well, then turn off the heat and let stand for a minute before serving.
From: danielh@sequent.com (Daniel Hobbs)
